entities.name: SCLEROSPORA GRAMINICOLA
entities.description: A fungal pathogen that causes disease in pearl millet

entities.name: VIRULENCE
entities.description: A qualitative measure of the relative capacity of an isolate to infect a host genotype

entities.name: AGGRESSIVENESS
entities.description: A quantitative measure of the infection causing potential of an isolate, calculated as disease incidence

entities.name: VIRULENCE INDEX
entities.description: A quantitative measure of virulence, measured as disease incidence multiplied by the inverse of the latent period

entities.name: PATHOTYPES
entities.description: Distinct groups of Sclerospora graminicola isolates with specific virulence patterns on host genotypes

entities.name: HOST-PATHOGEN SPECIFICITY
entities.description: The specific interaction between a pathogen pathotype and a host genotype

entities.name: RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of pearl millet genotypes to resist infection by Sclerospora graminicola

entities.name: GREENHOUSE
entities.description: The controlled environment where the virulence and aggressiveness evaluations were conducted

entities.name: ASEXUAL GENERATIONS
entities.description: The process of successive reproduction used to select specific pathotypes from a field population
